name: Admin Endpoint Protection Rules
description: Restricts access to sensitive administrative paths (e.g., /admin, /wp-admin, /dashboard, /manage) through layered controls: trusted IP allowlists, request rate limiting, and authentication/session signal validation.
objective: Reduce unauthorized access, brute-force attempts, and reconnaissance against administrative surfaces.
risk_mitigated: Credential stuffing and brute-force on admin interfaces
Privilege abuse via unauthenticated entry paths

rules.description: Match requests targeting sensitive admin paths.
Block admin-path traffic unless source IP is in a trusted allowlist.
tuning: Include VPN and office egress ranges; use short TTL for temporary entries.
If remote workforce is dynamic, prefer identity-aware controls as primary and IP allowlist as secondary.

rules.description: Apply strict per-IP request limits on admin paths to reduce brute-force and scanning activity.
tuning: Start in monitor/log mode and baseline legitimate admin behavior.
Use lower thresholds for login or auth endpoints nested under admin paths.
Consider separate thresholds for trusted automation accounts.

rules.description: Require valid authentication/session signals for admin-path requests; otherwise challenge or block.
tuning: Map auth signals to platform-native identity indicators (JWT validity, session cookie integrity, device trust).
For machine-to-machine admin APIs, replace MFA requirement with mTLS/client cert verification.
Escalate from challenge to block after confidence is established.

deployment_notes: Deploy in detection/log mode first and review at least 7 days of traffic.
Validate that break-glass and incident response admin access paths remain functional.
Roll out in stages: allowlist enforcement, then rate limiting, then auth-signal challenge/block.
Document operational ownership for allowlist maintenance and emergency exceptions.

metrics: Blocked admin-path requests by source IP and path
Auth-signal challenges and challenge solve rate
alerts: Spike in denied requests to /admin or /wp-admin
Sudden increase in failed auth-signal checks from previously trusted ranges

false_positive_considerations: Shared NAT egress can cause legitimate users to hit per-IP thresholds.
Some legacy admin tools may not present modern auth headers/signals.
Path-based matching should be reviewed for app-specific admin URL conventions.
